title: alangium villosum subsp. parviflorum
intro: Alangium villosum subsp. parviflorum, also known as the small-flowered alangium, is a plant species within the family Alangiaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Alangium villosum subsp. parviflorum include its potential to lower blood sugar levels, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
